acceptanceCriteria.ac: FirebaseService oluşturulmuş - src/modules/notifications/services/firebase.service.ts, Firebase Admin SDK configured, Environment variables: FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID, FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY, FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL, FIREBASE_ENABLED, Optional feature: Skip if FIREBASE_ENABLED=false

FirebaseService methods - sendPush(deviceToken: string, title: string, body: string, data?: object): Promise<void> method, Firebase Admin SDK kullanarak push notification gönderimi, Error handling: Invalid token → mark token as inactive, log error
DeviceToken entity oluşturulmuş - Fields: id, domainID, userID, token (unique), platform (iOS|Android), createdAt, Indexes: [domainID, userID], [token], Multi-tenancy support (domainID filtering)

POST /users/me/device-tokens endpoint - Register device token endpoint, Request DTO: token, platform (iOS|Android), Authentication: JwtAuthGuard required, User can only register own tokens (userID from JWT), Response: DeviceToken entity

NotificationService, PUSH channel için FirebaseService kullanıyor - NotificationService.send() method'unda PUSH channel enabled ise FirebaseService.sendPush() çağırılır, User'ın device token'ları fetch edilir, Her device token için push notification gönderilir, Notification history database'e kaydedilir (channel: PUSH, sent: true/false)

Error handling implemented - Invalid token → mark token as inactive (soft delete veya isActive flag), Firebase service unavailable → log error, don't block other channels, Partial success: Some tokens succeed, some fail → record individually

doc.title: Epic 5: Communication Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Firebase push notification support for mobile app. Firebase Admin SDK configured with environment variables. DeviceToken entity for token management. POST /users/me/device-tokens endpoint for token registration.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Communication Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-5.8.1 through AC-5.8.6). FirebaseService with sendPush method. DeviceToken entity with domainID, userID, token, platform. Error handling for invalid tokens.

doc.title: Story 5.7: Unified Notification Service
doc.snippet: NotificationService implementation with PUSH channel stub. Multi-channel sending pattern. Async fire-and-forget pattern. NotificationPreferencesService for channel filtering.

doc.title: Story 5.6: Notification Preferences Entity
doc.snippet: NotificationPreferencesService with getPreferences method. Default preferences: All channels enabled. Channel enum: EMAIL, SMS, PUSH.

doc.title: Implementation Patterns
doc.snippet: Controller-Service pattern: Controller handles HTTP layer, Service handles business logic. Multi-tenancy pattern with domainID filtering. Provider abstraction pattern for easy switching.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Unit tests for services (80%+ coverage). Mock external dependencies. Fast execution (no database, no network).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Multi-channel notification sending flow. User preferences filtering. Parallel channel execution. Partial success handling. Notification history tracking.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Provider abstraction pattern for easy provider switching. FirebaseService optional feature. Environment variable configuration (FIREBASE_ENABLED).

doc.title: Prisma Schema
doc.snippet: Notification entity with id, domainID, userID, type, channel, title, message, data (JSON), sent (boolean), sentAt, createdAt. Indexes on domainID, userID, type, sent.

doc.title: Prisma Schema
doc.snippet: NotificationPreference entity with id, domainID, userID, channel (EMAIL|SMS|PUSH), enabled (boolean). Unique constraint on [domainID, userID, channel].

artifact.reason: PUSH channel stub implementation that needs to be replaced with real FirebaseService integration. Currently creates Notification record with sent: false. Checks FIREBASE_ENABLED environment variable.

Main notification sending method that orchestrates multi-channel sending. Fetches user preferences, filters enabled channels, calls sendToChannel() for each channel. Uses async fire-and-forget pattern.
Email channel implementation pattern to follow for PUSH channel. Creates Notification record with sent status. Error handling with Sentry integration.
SMS channel implementation pattern to follow for PUSH channel. Partial success handling. Notification record creation. Error logging.
Helper method to create Notification records in database. Used by all channel methods. Tracks notification history with sent status and error messages.
Fetches user notification preferences. Returns array of NotificationPreference entities. Used by NotificationService to filter enabled channels.

Notification endpoints controller. POST /notifications/send endpoint uses fire-and-forget pattern. GET /users/me/notifications endpoint for history. Pattern to follow for device token registration endpoint.
Module configuration. Imports PrismaModule, MailModule, SmsModule. Exports NotificationService and NotificationPreferencesService. Needs FirebaseService and DeviceTokenService added.
Notification channel enum with EMAIL, SMS, PUSH values. Used throughout NotificationService for channel filtering.
Notification entity definition. Reference for DeviceToken entity structure. Multi-tenancy support with domainID. User relation.
NotificationPreference entity definition. Reference for DeviceToken entity structure. Unique constraint pattern. Multi-tenancy support.
Decorator to extract user from JWT token. Used in controllers for authenticated endpoints. Returns JwtPayload with sub (userID), domainID, roles.
JWT authentication guard. Protects routes requiring authentication. Used in NotificationController pattern.

package.name: firebase-admin
package.reason: Firebase Admin SDK for push notifications

constraints.constraint: Controller-Service Pattern: DeviceTokenController → DeviceTokenService → PrismaService pattern. NotificationService → FirebaseService → Firebase Admin SDK pattern. Controller handles HTTP layer, Service handles business logic.

Multi-Tenancy Pattern: @DomainID decorator + domainID filtering in all queries. DeviceToken entity includes domainID field. All queries filtered by domainID from JWT token. User can only access own device tokens (userID from JWT).

Provider Abstraction Pattern: FirebaseService uses provider abstraction pattern. NotificationService injects FirebaseService. Provider switching easy via environment variables. Optional feature: FIREBASE_ENABLED=false skips Firebase.

Unified Notification Pattern: NotificationService filters channels by user preferences. Multi-channel sending: EMAIL, SMS, PUSH channels parallel. PUSH channel: Fetch user device tokens, send to each token. Partial success: One token failure doesn't block others. History tracking: Each attempt recorded in Notification entity.

Async Fire-and-Forget Pattern: Push notification sending uses async fire-and-forget (no await, catch error handling). Communication operations don't block other flows. Error handling: Log errors but don't throw exceptions.

Error Handling Pattern: Partial success OK - one device token failure doesn't block others. Invalid token → mark as inactive, don't block other tokens. Failure → log to Sentry. Notification record → sent: false for failures.

Testing Requirements: Unit tests for FirebaseService (80%+ coverage). Unit tests for DeviceTokenService (80%+ coverage). Integration tests for endpoints. E2E tests for full flow. Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Mock external dependencies (Firebase Admin SDK).

Coding Standards: Follow existing NotificationService patterns. Use same error handling approach as EMAIL/SMS channels. Use same Notification record creation pattern. Follow existing DTO validation patterns. Use existing Swagger decorators.

interface.name: POST /users/me/device-tokens
interface.signature: POST /users/me/device-tokens - Register device token for push notifications. Request: RegisterDeviceTokenDto { token: string, platform: Platform enum }. Response: DeviceTokenResDto { id, token, platform, createdAt }. Auth: JwtAuthGuard required.

interface.name: NotificationService.send()
interface.signature: async send(userID: string, domainID: string, type: NotificationType, title: string, message: string, data?: object): Promise<void>

interface.name: FirebaseService.sendPush()
interface.signature: async sendPush(deviceToken: string, title: string, body: string, data?: object): Promise<void>

interface.name: DeviceTokenService.registerToken()
interface.signature: async registerToken(userID: string, domainID: string, token: string, platform: Platform): Promise<DeviceToken>

interface.name: DeviceTokenService.getUserTokens()
interface.signature: async getUserTokens(userID: string, domainID: string): Promise<DeviceToken[]>

interface.name: NotificationPreferencesService.getPreferences()
interface.signature: async getPreferences(userID: string, domainID: string): Promise<NotificationPreference[]>

tests.standards: Testing follows Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Unit tests mock external dependencies (Firebase Admin SDK, PrismaService). Integration tests use real database (test database). E2E tests use supertest for HTTP requests. Coverage targets: Services 80%+, Controllers 70%+. Test isolation: Each test independent, no shared state. Mock strategy: External services always mocked, internal services mocked in unit tests, real in E2E tests.
tests.locations: Unit tests: src/modules/notifications/services/*.spec.ts. Integration tests: test/notifications-integration.e2e-spec.ts. E2E tests: test/notifications.e2e-spec.ts. Test setup: test/jest-e2e-setup.ts, test/utils/seedTestData.ts.

ideas.test: Unit test FirebaseService initialization - Firebase Admin SDK configured correctly, Environment variables loaded, FIREBASE_ENABLED=false skips initialization
Unit test FirebaseService.sendPush() - Success case with valid token, Invalid token error handling, Network error handling, FIREBASE_ENABLED=false skip
Unit test DeviceToken entity - Prisma schema validation, Field validation, Index validation, Unique constraint validation

Unit test DeviceTokenService.registerToken() - Success case, Duplicate token handling, Multi-tenancy filtering, Unit test DeviceTokenService.getUserTokens() - Returns user tokens, DomainID filtering, Unit test DeviceTokenService.invalidateToken() - Marks token as inactive
Integration test POST /users/me/device-tokens - Registers device token (authenticated), Duplicate token handling, Multi-tenancy filtering (domainID), Invalid platform enum

Unit test NotificationService.send() - PUSH channel enabled with single device token, PUSH channel enabled with multiple device tokens, PUSH channel enabled with partial success (one token fails), PUSH channel disabled (preferences)
Integration test NotificationService.send() - Multi-channel with PUSH enabled, PUSH channel with invalid token handling
Unit test Error handling - Invalid token marks token as inactive, Firebase unavailable doesn't block other channels, Partial success records individually

E2E test Push notification flow - Register device token → Send notification → Verify history, Multi-channel notification (EMAIL, SMS, PUSH all enabled), Invalid token → Token invalidated, notification history recorded
